text:
Andy Murrayreceived an apology immediately after entering retirement at the Olympics. In his final tournament, the former world No. 1 paired up withDan Evansin the men’s doubles event and made it all the way to the quarter-final.

But theysuffered a one-sided 6-1 6-4 defeatto American duo Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul on Thursday night, just missing out on the medal rounds.

After the match, Fritz confessed that he said sorry to Murray at the net and didn’t take pride in being the one to end the three-time Grand Slam champion’s career.

“Obviously we want to win like we were pumped up to win. But it is sad I guess to see it end for Andy, he's someone that I think both of us have looked up to for a very long time,” the world No. 12 said.

“He's a great guy so I guess I don’t take like, a lot of pride in doing it. But you know, we're here. We're here to try and win a medal.” It was an emotional night on Court Suzanne Lenglen, withboth Murray and Evans in tears.

And it was a strange feeling for the Americans, who struggled to celebrate as usual. Sharing what he told Murray at the net, Fritz added: “I just said like, I don’t even know what, I feel like I said like, sorry. And like and also like congrats on the career but I don't really know what to say to him.”

Just in:Carlos Alcaraz thrashes Auger-Aliassime as Spaniard one win from Olympic gold

They were in a similar position at the 2022 Laver Cup -Roger Federer’s retirement tournament. The Swiss star teamed up withRafael Nadalin doubles but they lost to Team World’s Jack Sock and Frances Tiafoe. Fritz and Paul were also part of the rival squad and World went on to lift the trophy at the end of the weekend.

“Laver Cup with Roger was a similar feeling, we won but it was like sad because he was gone and we're all gonna miss Andy a ton,” Paul explained. Murray refused to go down without one last fightback on Thursday night.

He and Evans had saved multiple match points in the first two rounds. And Murray saved one more with a smash winner when Paul served for a spot in the semi-final while leading 5-2. The Brits broke back before Fritz got the job done, and the Americans were relieved to have had a double-break lead.

“I was hoping [the comeback] wasn't coming when I was gonna get another chance to serve it out. Even if we didn't get it done there,” Fritz laughed. “That's why we tried really hard to be up to double break. And also both of us know it's just a better combo with me serving and Tommy at the net than him serving and me at the net. So we felt pretty comfortable serving that one out at the last game.”

Fritz has come a long way since his first encounter with Murray, when he was a teenager invited to practice with the two-time gold medallist at the ATP Finals when they were held in London. Recalling their initial meeting, he said: “I think it might have been when I was going to the World Tour Finals like as a 17-year-old.

“I think I was accepting something I think for being the youngest in the top 100 and I was warming him up. It was crazy because I was jet lagged and he wanted to practice at like 8am every morning. So I was practising with him at 8am every morning for World Tour Finals because he was getting ready to play Cilic so I was like his practice partner for that.”

title: Andy Murray gets immediate apology after retiring at Olympics as rival shares net exchange | Tennis | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Yasmin Syed
description: Andy Murray officially retired when he and Dan Evans lost to Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul at the Olympics.
